---
title: "Other sites"
page-layout: article
format:
  html:
    include-before-body: _lfl-nav.html
---

If you are still considering whether to change to LaTeX, you might be interested in

- [A philosophy blog exchange](http://philosophysmoker.blogspot.co.uk/2010/06/title-latex.html) about the pros and cons.

Next, here is another discipline-related LaTeX site that might be of particular interest to logicians and philosophers:

- [LaTeX for Linguists](http://www.essex.ac.uk/linguistics/external/clmt/latex4ling/) is an extensive site comparable with LaTeX for Logicians.

TeXies will already know about

- [The Comprehensive TeX Archive](http://ctan.org/), the place to get everything LaTeX-related you want (and a vast amount you probably will never want too).

- [A TeX and LaTeX related "StackExchange"](http://tex.stackexchange.com/) (that means a question and answer forum like the wonderful [MathOverflow](http://mathoverflow.net/)).

- The Wikibooks [LaTeX Wiki](http://en.wikibooks.org/wiki/LaTeX).

*Links checked 19 October 2014*
